authors_text: Ruiqi Xian,Xijun Wang,Dinesh Manocha
title: MITFAS: Mutual Information based Temporal Feature Alignment and Sampling for Aerial Video Action Recognition
title_zh: MITFAS：基于互信息的时间特征对齐和采样，用于航空视频动作识别
abstract: We present a novel approach for action recognition in UAV videos. Our formulation is designed to handle occlusion and viewpoint changes caused by the movement of a UAV. We use the concept of mutual information to compute and align the regions corresponding to human action or motion in the temporal domain. This enables our recognition model to learn from the key features associated with the motion. We also propose a novel frame sampling method that uses joint mutual information to acquire the most informative frame sequence in UAV videos. We have integrated our approach with X3D and evaluated the performance on multiple datasets. In practice, we achieve 18.9% improvement in Top-1 accuracy over current state-of-the-art methods on UAV-Human(Li et al., 2021), 7.3% improvement on Drone-Action(Perera et al., 2019), and 7.16% improvement on NEC Drones(Choi et al., 2020).
summary: We present a novel approach for action recognition in UAV videos. Our formulation is designed to handle occlusion and viewpoint changes caused by the movement of a UAV. We use the concept of mutual information to compute and align the regions corresponding to human action or motion in the temporal domain. This enables our recognition model to learn from the key features associated with the motion. We also propose a novel frame sampling method that uses joint mutual information to acquire the most informative frame sequence in UAV videos. We have integrated our approach with X3D and evaluated the performance on multiple datasets. In practice, we achieve 18.9% improvement in Top-1 accuracy over current state-of-the-art methods on UAV-Human(Li et al., 2021), 7.3% improvement on Drone-Action(Perera et al., 2019), and 7.16% improvement on NEC Drones(Choi et al., 2020).
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种无人机视频中动作识别的新颖方法。我们的公式旨在处理无人机运动引起的遮挡和视点变化。我们使用互信息的概念来计算和对齐时域中与人类动作或运动相对应的区域。这使得我们的识别模型能够学习与运动相关的关键特征。我们还提出了一种新颖的帧采样方法，该方法使用联合互信息来获取无人机视频中信息最丰富的帧序列。我们将我们的方法与 X3D 集成，并评估了多个数据集的性能。在实践中，我们在 UAV-Human 上实现了 Top-1 精度比当前最先进的方法提高了 18.9%（Li et al., 2021），在 Drone-Action 上实现了 7.3% 的提高（Perera et al., 2019），在 NEC Drones 上实现了 7.16% 的提高（Choi et al., 2020）。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于互信息的时序特征对齐与采样方法，用于无人机视频中的动作识别，以应对遮挡和视角变化带来的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理无人机视频中的遮挡和视角变化时存在局限性，需提升动作识别的准确性和鲁棒性。
AI.method: 基于互信息的时序特征对齐与采样方法，通过计算动作区域的互信息进行对齐，并采用联合互信息选择信息量最大的帧序列，结合X3D模型进行识别。
AI.result: 在UAV-Human、Drone-Action和NEC Drones数据集上分别提升Top-1准确率18.9%、7.3%和7.16%，优于现有最先进的方法。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的方法有效应对无人机视频中的遮挡和视角变化，显著提升动作识别性能，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Dezhong Tong,Andrew Choi,Longhui Qin,Weicheng Huang,Jungseock Joo,M. Khalid Jawed
title: Sim2Real Neural Controllers for Physics-based Robotic Deployment of Deformable Linear Objects
title_zh: Sim2Real 神经控制器用于可变形线性物体的基于物理的机器人部署
abstract: Deformable linear objects (DLOs), such as rods, cables, and ropes, play important roles in daily life. However, manipulation of DLOs is challenging as large geometrically nonlinear deformations may occur during the manipulation process. This problem is made even more difficult as the different deformation modes (e.g., stretching, bending, and twisting) may result in elastic instabilities during manipulation. In this paper, we formulate a physics-guided data-driven method to solve a challenging manipulation task -- accurately deploying a DLO (an elastic rod) onto a rigid substrate along various prescribed patterns. Our framework combines machine learning, scaling analysis, and physical simulations to develop a physics-based neural controller for deployment. We explore the complex interplay between the gravitational and elastic energies of the manipulated DLO and obtain a control method for DLO deployment that is robust against friction and material properties. Out of the numerous geometrical and material properties of the rod and substrate, we show that only three non-dimensional parameters are needed to describe the deployment process with physical analysis. Therefore, the essence of the controlling law for the manipulation task can be constructed with a low-dimensional model, drastically increasing the computation speed. The effectiveness of our optimal control scheme is shown through a comprehensive robotic case study comparing against a heuristic control method for deploying rods for a wide variety of patterns. In addition to this, we also showcase the practicality of our control scheme by having a robot accomplish challenging high-level tasks such as mimicking human handwriting, cable placement, and tying knots.
summary: Deformable linear objects (DLOs), such as rods, cables, and ropes, play important roles in daily life. However, manipulation of DLOs is challenging as large geometrically nonlinear deformations may occur during the manipulation process. This problem is made even more difficult as the different deformation modes (e.g., stretching, bending, and twisting) may result in elastic instabilities during manipulation. In this paper, we formulate a physics-guided data-driven method to solve a challenging manipulation task -- accurately deploying a DLO (an elastic rod) onto a rigid substrate along various prescribed patterns. Our framework combines machine learning, scaling analysis, and physical simulations to develop a physics-based neural controller for deployment. We explore the complex interplay between the gravitational and elastic energies of the manipulated DLO and obtain a control method for DLO deployment that is robust against friction and material properties. Out of the numerous geometrical and material properties of the rod and substrate, we show that only three non-dimensional parameters are needed to describe the deployment process with physical analysis. Therefore, the essence of the controlling law for the manipulation task can be constructed with a low-dimensional model, drastically increasing the computation speed. The effectiveness of our optimal control scheme is shown through a comprehensive robotic case study comparing against a heuristic control method for deploying rods for a wide variety of patterns. In addition to this, we also showcase the practicality of our control scheme by having a robot accomplish challenging high-level tasks such as mimicking human handwriting, cable placement, and tying knots.
abstract_zh: 可变形线性物体（DLO），例如杆、电缆和绳索，在日常生活中发挥着重要作用。然而，DLO 的操纵具有挑战性，因为在操纵过程中可能会发生较大的几何非线性变形。这个问题变得更加困难，因为不同的变形模式（例如拉伸、弯曲和扭曲）可能导致操作过程中的弹性不稳定。在本文中，我们制定了一种物理引导的数据驱动方法来解决具有挑战性的操纵任务——沿着各种规定的图案将 DLO（弹性杆）准确地部署到刚性基板上。我们的框架结合了机器学习、缩放分析和物理模拟来开发基于物理的神经控制器进行部署。我们探索了被操纵的 DLO 的重力和弹性能之间复杂的相互作用，并获得了一种对摩擦和材料特性具有鲁棒性的 DLO 部署控制方法。在杆和基底的众多几何和材料特性中，我们表明只需要三个无量纲参数来通过物理分析来描述部署过程。因此，操纵任务的控制律的本质可以用低维模型来构建，从而大大提高计算速度。我们的最优控制方案的有效性通过全面的机器人案例研究与用于部署各种模式的杆的启发式控制方法进行比较来证明。除此之外，我们还通过让机器人完成具有挑战性的高级任务（例如模仿人类笔迹、电缆放置和打结）来展示我们的控制方案的实用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物理引导的神经控制器，用于将可变形线性物体（如弹性杆）精确部署到刚性基底上，解决其在操作过程中出现的几何非线性变形和弹性失稳问题。
AI.motivation: 可变形线性物体（DLOs）在操作过程中易产生复杂的几何非线性变形和弹性失稳，传统方法难以应对不同摩擦力和材料属性的鲁棒性需求，亟需一种结合物理规律与数据驱动的控制方案。
AI.method: 融合机器学习、尺度分析与物理仿真，构建基于物理的神经控制器。通过分析重力与弹性能量的相互作用，提取三个无量纲参数描述部署过程，建立低维模型以提升计算效率。
AI.result: 实验表明，该控制方案在多种复杂模式（如模仿手写、电缆铺设、打结）中优于启发式方法，且能有效适应不同材料属性和摩擦条件。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的低维物理驱动控制模型，为可变形线性物体的高精度部署提供了高效且鲁棒的解决方案，验证了其在实际机器人任务中的可行性。

search_text: Sim2Real Neural Controllers for Physics-based Robotic Deployment of Deformable Linear Objects Sim2Real 神经控制器用于可变形线性物体的基于物理的机器人部署 Deformable linear objects (DLOs), such as rods, cables, and ropes, play important roles in daily life. However, manipulation of DLOs is challenging as large geometrically nonlinear deformations may occur during the manipulation process. This problem is made even more difficult as the different deformation modes (e.g., stretching, bending, and twisting) may result in elastic instabilities during manipulation. In this paper, we formulate a physics-guided data-driven method to solve a challenging manipulation task -- accurately deploying a DLO (an elastic rod) onto a rigid substrate along various prescribed patterns. Our framework combines machine learning, scaling analysis, and physical simulations to develop a physics-based neural controller for deployment. We explore the complex interplay between the gravitational and elastic energies of the manipulated DLO and obtain a control method for DLO deployment that is robust against friction and material properties. Out of the numerous geometrical and material properties of the rod and substrate, we show that only three non-dimensional parameters are needed to describe the deployment process with physical analysis. Therefore, the essence of the controlling law for the manipulation task can be constructed with a low-dimensional model, drastically increasing the computation speed. The effectiveness of our optimal control scheme is shown through a comprehensive robotic case study comparing against a heuristic control method for deploying rods for a wide variety of patterns. In addition to this, we also showcase the practicality of our control scheme by having a robot accomplish challenging high-level tasks such as mimicking human handwriting, cable placement, and tying knots. 可变形线性物体（DLO），例如杆、电缆和绳索，在日常生活中发挥着重要作用。然而，DLO 的操纵具有挑战性，因为在操纵过程中可能会发生较大的几何非线性变形。这个问题变得更加困难，因为不同的变形模式（例如拉伸、弯曲和扭曲）可能导致操作过程中的弹性不稳定。在本文中，我们制定了一种物理引导的数据驱动方法来解决具有挑战性的操纵任务——沿着各种规定的图案将 DLO（弹性杆）准确地部署到刚性基板上。我们的框架结合了机器学习、缩放分析和物理模拟来开发基于物理的神经控制器进行部署。我们探索了被操纵的 DLO 的重力和弹性能之间复杂的相互作用，并获得了一种对摩擦和材料特性具有鲁棒性的 DLO 部署控制方法。在杆和基底的众多几何和材料特性中，我们表明只需要三个无量纲参数来通过物理分析来描述部署过程。因此，操纵任务的控制律的本质可以用低维模型来构建，从而大大提高计算速度。我们的最优控制方案的有效性通过全面的机器人案例研究与用于部署各种模式的杆的启发式控制方法进行比较来证明。除此之外，我们还通过让机器人完成具有挑战性的高级任务（例如模仿人类笔迹、电缆放置和打结）来展示我们的控制方案的实用性。 本文提出了一种基于物理引导的神经控制器，用于将可变形线性物体（如弹性杆）精确部署到刚性基底上，解决其在操作过程中出现的几何非线性变形和弹性失稳问题。 Dezhong Tong,Andrew Choi,Longhui Qin,Weicheng Huang,Jungseock Joo,M. Khalid Jawed cs.RO,physics.app-ph

authors_text: Hao Liu,Muhan Zhang,Zehao Dong,Lecheng Kong,Yixin Chen,Bradley Fritz,Dacheng Tao,Christopher King
title: Time Associated Meta Learning for Clinical Prediction
title_zh: 用于临床预测的时间相关元学习
abstract: Rich Electronic Health Records (EHR), have created opportunities to improve clinical processes using machine learning methods. Prediction of the same patient events at different time horizons can have very different applications and interpretations; however, limited number of events in each potential time window hurts the effectiveness of conventional machine learning algorithms. We propose a novel time associated meta learning (TAML) method to make effective predictions at multiple future time points. We view time-associated disease prediction as classification tasks at multiple time points. Such closely-related classification tasks are an excellent candidate for model-based meta learning. To address the sparsity problem after task splitting, TAML employs a temporal information sharing strategy to augment the number of positive samples and include the prediction of related phenotypes or events in the meta-training phase. We demonstrate the effectiveness of TAML on multiple clinical datasets, where it consistently outperforms a range of strong baselines. We also develop a MetaEHR package for implementing both time-associated and time-independent few-shot prediction on EHR data.
summary: Rich Electronic Health Records (EHR), have created opportunities to improve clinical processes using machine learning methods. Prediction of the same patient events at different time horizons can have very different applications and interpretations; however, limited number of events in each potential time window hurts the effectiveness of conventional machine learning algorithms. We propose a novel time associated meta learning (TAML) method to make effective predictions at multiple future time points. We view time-associated disease prediction as classification tasks at multiple time points. Such closely-related classification tasks are an excellent candidate for model-based meta learning. To address the sparsity problem after task splitting, TAML employs a temporal information sharing strategy to augment the number of positive samples and include the prediction of related phenotypes or events in the meta-training phase. We demonstrate the effectiveness of TAML on multiple clinical datasets, where it consistently outperforms a range of strong baselines. We also develop a MetaEHR package for implementing both time-associated and time-independent few-shot prediction on EHR data.
abstract_zh: 丰富的电子健康记录 (EHR) 为使用机器学习方法改进临床流程创造了机会。对不同时间范围内相同患者事件的预测可能有非常不同的应用和解释；然而，每个潜在时间窗口中的事件数量有限会损害传统机器学习算法的有效性。我们提出了一种新颖的时间相关元学习（TAML）方法，可以在多个未来时间点做出有效的预测。我们将与时间相关的疾病预测视为多个时间点的分类任务。这种密切相关的分类任务是基于模型的元学习的绝佳候选者。为了解决任务分割后的稀疏问题，TAML 采用时间信息共享策略来增加正样本的数量，并在元训练阶段包含对相关表型或事件的预测。我们在多个临床数据集上证明了 TAML 的有效性，它始终优于一系列强大的基线。我们还开发了一个 MetaEHR 包，用于对 EHR 数据实施时间相关和时间无关的小样本预测。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种时间关联元学习方法（TAML），用于利用电子健康记录（EHR）数据在多个未来时间点进行临床事件预测。
AI.motivation: 传统机器学习方法在处理不同时间窗口内事件数量稀疏的问题时效果受限，需开发能有效整合多时间点信息的预测方法。
AI.method: 将多时间点疾病预测视为相关分类任务，通过元学习框架共享时间信息，利用关联表型/事件预测增强正样本数量，解决任务分割后的稀疏性问题。
AI.result: 在多个临床数据集上验证了TAML的一致有效性，优于多种强基线方法，并开发了支持时间关联和非关联少样本预测的MetaEHR工具包。
AI.conclusion: TAML通过时间信息共享策略有效解决了EHR数据稀疏性问题，为多时间点临床预测提供了可行方案并提供了实用工具。

authors_text: Yixin Liu,Chenrui Fan,Pan Zhou,Lichao Sun
title: Unlearnable Graph: Protecting Graphs from Unauthorized Exploitation
title_zh: 无法学习的图：保护图免遭未经授权的利用
comment: This paper is accepted as a poster for NDSS 2023
comments: This paper is accepted as a poster for NDSS 2023
abstract: While the use of graph-structured data in various fields is becoming increasingly popular, it also raises concerns about the potential unauthorized exploitation of personal data for training commercial graph neural network (GNN) models, which can compromise privacy. To address this issue, we propose a novel method for generating unlearnable graph examples. By injecting delusive but imperceptible noise into graphs using our Error-Minimizing Structural Poisoning (EMinS) module, we are able to make the graphs unexploitable. Notably, by modifying only $5\%$ at most of the potential edges in the graph data, our method successfully decreases the accuracy from ${77.33\%}$ to ${42.47\%}$ on the COLLAB dataset.
summary: While the use of graph-structured data in various fields is becoming increasingly popular, it also raises concerns about the potential unauthorized exploitation of personal data for training commercial graph neural network (GNN) models, which can compromise privacy. To address this issue, we propose a novel method for generating unlearnable graph examples. By injecting delusive but imperceptible noise into graphs using our Error-Minimizing Structural Poisoning (EMinS) module, we are able to make the graphs unexploitable. Notably, by modifying only $5\%$ at most of the potential edges in the graph data, our method successfully decreases the accuracy from ${77.33\%}$ to ${42.47\%}$ on the COLLAB dataset.
abstract_zh: 虽然图结构数据在各个领域的使用变得越来越流行，但它也引起了人们对个人数据可能未经授权的利用来训练商业图神经网络（GNN）模型的担忧，这可能会损害隐私。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种生成不可学习的图示例的新方法。通过使用我们的误差最小化结构中毒 (EMinS) 模块向图表中注入欺骗性但难以察觉的噪声，我们能够使图表无法被利用。值得注意的是，通过仅修改图数据中大多数潜在边的 $5\%$，我们的方法成功地将 COLLAB 数据集上的精度从 ${77.33\%}$ 降低到 ${42.47\%}$。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种生成不可学习图数据的方法，通过注入微小噪声防止图神经网络模型未经授权地利用图结构数据。
AI.motivation: 图数据的广泛应用引发隐私泄露风险，现有方法难以有效阻止商业GNN模型窃取敏感图数据进行训练。
AI.method: 设计误差最小化结构投毒模块（EMinS），在图中注入人眼不可察的噪声，仅修改最多5%的潜在边结构。
AI.result: 在COLLAB数据集上将GNN模型准确率从77.33%降至42.47%，验证了方法在保护隐私方面的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过少量结构修改即可显著降低模型性能，为图数据隐私保护提供了可行的技术方案。

tldr: 本文提出一种生成不可学习图数据的方法，通过注入微小噪声防止图神经网络模型未经授权地利用图结构数据。
motivation: 图数据的广泛应用引发隐私泄露风险，现有方法难以有效阻止商业GNN模型窃取敏感图数据进行训练。
method: 设计误差最小化结构投毒模块（EMinS），在图中注入人眼不可察的噪声，仅修改最多5%的潜在边结构。
result: 在COLLAB数据集上将GNN模型准确率从77.33%降至42.47%，验证了方法在保护隐私方面的有效性。
conclusion: 该方法通过少量结构修改即可显著降低模型性能，为图数据隐私保护提供了可行的技术方案。
search_text: Unlearnable Graph: Protecting Graphs from Unauthorized Exploitation 无法学习的图：保护图免遭未经授权的利用 While the use of graph-structured data in various fields is becoming increasingly popular, it also raises concerns about the potential unauthorized exploitation of personal data for training commercial graph neural network (GNN) models, which can compromise privacy. To address this issue, we propose a novel method for generating unlearnable graph examples. By injecting delusive but imperceptible noise into graphs using our Error-Minimizing Structural Poisoning (EMinS) module, we are able to make the graphs unexploitable. Notably, by modifying only $5\%$ at most of the potential edges in the graph data, our method successfully decreases the accuracy from ${77.33\%}$ to ${42.47\%}$ on the COLLAB dataset. 虽然图结构数据在各个领域的使用变得越来越流行，但它也引起了人们对个人数据可能未经授权的利用来训练商业图神经网络（GNN）模型的担忧，这可能会损害隐私。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种生成不可学习的图示例的新方法。通过使用我们的误差最小化结构中毒 (EMinS) 模块向图表中注入欺骗性但难以察觉的噪声，我们能够使图表无法被利用。值得注意的是，通过仅修改图数据中大多数潜在边的 $5\%$，我们的方法成功地将 COLLAB 数据集上的精度从 ${77.33\%}$ 降低到 ${42.47\%}$。 本文提出一种生成不可学习图数据的方法，通过注入微小噪声防止图神经网络模型未经授权地利用图结构数据。 Yixin Liu,Chenrui Fan,Pan Zhou,Lichao Sun cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR

authors_text: Yaxuan Liu,Jian Jin,Yuan Xue,Weisi Lin
title: The First Comprehensive Dataset with Multiple Distortion Types for Visual Just-Noticeable Differences
title_zh: 第一个具有多种失真类型的综合数据集，可实现视觉上的明显差异
abstract: Recently, with the development of deep learning, a number of Just Noticeable Difference (JND) datasets have been built for JND modeling. However, all the existing JND datasets only label the JND points based on the level of compression distortion. Hence, JND models learned from such datasets can only be used for image/video compression. As known, JND is a major characteristic of the human visual system (HVS), which reflects the maximum visual distortion that the HVS can tolerate. Hence, a generalized JND modeling should take more kinds of distortion types into account. To benefit JND modeling, this work establishes a generalized JND dataset with a coarse-to-fine JND selection, which contains 106 source images and 1,642 JND maps, covering 25 distortion types. To this end, we proposed a coarse JND candidate selection scheme to select the distorted images from the existing Image Quality Assessment (IQA) datasets as JND candidates instead of generating JND maps ourselves. Then, a fine JND selection is carried out on the JND candidates with a crowdsourced subjective assessment.
summary: Recently, with the development of deep learning, a number of Just Noticeable Difference (JND) datasets have been built for JND modeling. However, all the existing JND datasets only label the JND points based on the level of compression distortion. Hence, JND models learned from such datasets can only be used for image/video compression. As known, JND is a major characteristic of the human visual system (HVS), which reflects the maximum visual distortion that the HVS can tolerate. Hence, a generalized JND modeling should take more kinds of distortion types into account. To benefit JND modeling, this work establishes a generalized JND dataset with a coarse-to-fine JND selection, which contains 106 source images and 1,642 JND maps, covering 25 distortion types. To this end, we proposed a coarse JND candidate selection scheme to select the distorted images from the existing Image Quality Assessment (IQA) datasets as JND candidates instead of generating JND maps ourselves. Then, a fine JND selection is carried out on the JND candidates with a crowdsourced subjective assessment.
abstract_zh: 近年来，随着深度学习的发展，已经建立了许多可注意到的差异（JND）数据集用于JND建模。然而，所有现有的 JND 数据集仅根据压缩失真级别来标记 JND 点。因此，从此类数据集中学习的 JND 模型只能用于图像/视频压缩。众所周知，JND是人类视觉系统（HVS）的一个主要特征，反映了HVS能够容忍的最大视觉失真。因此，广义的 JND 建模应该考虑更多种类的失真类型。为了有利于JND建模，本文建立了一个由粗到细的JND选择的广义JND数据集，其中包含106张源图像和1,642张JND图，涵盖25种畸变类型。为此，我们提出了一种粗略的 JND 候选选择方案，从现有的图像质量评估（IQA）数据集中选择失真图像作为 JND 候选，而不是自己生成 JND 地图。然后，通过众包主观评估，对JND候选者进行精细的JND选择。

AI.tldr: 构建包含多种失真类型的首个全面视觉可察觉差异（JND）数据集。
AI.motivation: 现有JND数据集仅标注压缩失真下的JND点，导致模型仅适用于图像/视频压缩，无法反映人眼对多种失真类型的容忍度。
AI.method: 提出粗到细的JND筛选方案：首先从现有IQA数据集中选取失真候选图，再通过众包主观评估筛选最终JND图。
AI.result: 构建包含106幅源图像、1,642张JND图、覆盖25种失真类型的通用JND数据集。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集为建立更通用的JND模型提供了基础，有助于提升视觉系统对多类型失真的建模能力。

authors_text: Yixin Liu,Haohui Ye,Kai Zhang,Lichao Sun
title: Securing Biomedical Images from Unauthorized Training with Anti-Learning Perturbation
title_zh: 通过反学习扰动保护生物医学图像免遭未经授权的训练
comment: This paper is accepted as a poster for NDSS 2023
comments: This paper is accepted as a poster for NDSS 2023
abstract: The volume of open-source biomedical data has been essential to the development of various spheres of the healthcare community since more `free' data can provide individual researchers more chances to contribute. However, institutions often hesitate to share their data with the public due to the risk of data exploitation by unauthorized third parties for another commercial usage (e.g., training AI models). This phenomenon might hinder the development of the whole healthcare research community. To address this concern, we propose a novel approach termed `unlearnable biomedical image' for protecting biomedical data by injecting imperceptible but delusive noises into the data, making them unexploitable for AI models. We formulate the problem as a bi-level optimization and propose three kinds of anti-learning perturbation generation approaches to solve the problem. Our method is an important step toward encouraging more institutions to contribute their data for the long-term development of the research community.
summary: The volume of open-source biomedical data has been essential to the development of various spheres of the healthcare community since more `free' data can provide individual researchers more chances to contribute. However, institutions often hesitate to share their data with the public due to the risk of data exploitation by unauthorized third parties for another commercial usage (e.g., training AI models). This phenomenon might hinder the development of the whole healthcare research community. To address this concern, we propose a novel approach termed `unlearnable biomedical image' for protecting biomedical data by injecting imperceptible but delusive noises into the data, making them unexploitable for AI models. We formulate the problem as a bi-level optimization and propose three kinds of anti-learning perturbation generation approaches to solve the problem. Our method is an important step toward encouraging more institutions to contribute their data for the long-term development of the research community.
abstract_zh: 大量的开源生物医学数据对于医疗保健社区各个领域的发展至关重要，因为更多的“免费”数据可以为个体研究人员提供更多的贡献机会。然而，由于未经授权的第三方将数据用于其他商业用途（例如训练人工智能模型）的风险，机构常常犹豫是否与公众共享其数据。这种现象可能会阻碍整个医疗保健研究界的发展。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种名为“不可学习的生物医学图像”的新方法，通过向数据中注入难以察觉但具有欺骗性的噪声来保护生物医学数据，使它们无法被人工智能模型利用。我们将该问题表述为双层优化，并提出了三种反学习扰动生成方法来解决该问题。我们的方法是鼓励更多机构为研究界的长期发展贡献数据的重要一步。

AI.tldr: 提出一种通过添加不可察觉的欺骗性噪声来保护生物医学图像免受未经授权的AI模型训练的方法。
AI.motivation: 机构因担心数据被第三方用于商业AI训练而犹豫分享数据，阻碍了医疗研究社区的发展。
AI.method: 将问题建模为双层优化问题，提出三种生成反学习扰动的方法，通过注入难以察觉的噪声使数据对AI模型不可用。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能有效防止未经授权的AI模型利用生物医学图像数据，同时保持图像的可感知性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为鼓励机构共享数据、促进医疗研究社区长期发展提供了重要步骤。

authors_text: Wenxin Jiang,Nicholas Synovic,Matt Hyatt,Taylor R. Schorlemmer,Rohan Sethi,Yung-Hsiang Lu,George K. Thiruvathukal,James C. Davis
title: An Empirical Study of Pre-Trained Model Reuse in the Hugging Face Deep Learning Model Registry
title_zh: 拥抱脸深度学习模型注册表中预训练模型重用的实证研究
comment: Proceedings of the ACM/IEEE 45th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE) 2023
comments: Proceedings of the ACM/IEEE 45th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE) 2023
abstract: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are being adopted as components in software systems. Creating and specializing DNNs from scratch has grown increasingly difficult as state-of-the-art architectures grow more complex. Following the path of traditional software engineering, machine learning engineers have begun to reuse large-scale pre-trained models (PTMs) and fine-tune these models for downstream tasks. Prior works have studied reuse practices for traditional software packages to guide software engineers towards better package maintenance and dependency management. We lack a similar foundation of knowledge to guide behaviors in pre-trained model ecosystems. In this work, we present the first empirical investigation of PTM reuse. We interviewed 12 practitioners from the most popular PTM ecosystem, Hugging Face, to learn the practices and challenges of PTM reuse. From this data, we model the decision-making process for PTM reuse. Based on the identified practices, we describe useful attributes for model reuse, including provenance, reproducibility, and portability. Three challenges for PTM reuse are missing attributes, discrepancies between claimed and actual performance, and model risks. We substantiate these identified challenges with systematic measurements in the Hugging Face ecosystem. Our work informs future directions on optimizing deep learning ecosystems by automated measuring useful attributes and potential attacks, and envision future research on infrastructure and standardization for model registries.
summary: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are being adopted as components in software systems. Creating and specializing DNNs from scratch has grown increasingly difficult as state-of-the-art architectures grow more complex. Following the path of traditional software engineering, machine learning engineers have begun to reuse large-scale pre-trained models (PTMs) and fine-tune these models for downstream tasks. Prior works have studied reuse practices for traditional software packages to guide software engineers towards better package maintenance and dependency management. We lack a similar foundation of knowledge to guide behaviors in pre-trained model ecosystems. In this work, we present the first empirical investigation of PTM reuse. We interviewed 12 practitioners from the most popular PTM ecosystem, Hugging Face, to learn the practices and challenges of PTM reuse. From this data, we model the decision-making process for PTM reuse. Based on the identified practices, we describe useful attributes for model reuse, including provenance, reproducibility, and portability. Three challenges for PTM reuse are missing attributes, discrepancies between claimed and actual performance, and model risks. We substantiate these identified challenges with systematic measurements in the Hugging Face ecosystem. Our work informs future directions on optimizing deep learning ecosystems by automated measuring useful attributes and potential attacks, and envision future research on infrastructure and standardization for model registries.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 正在被用作软件系统的组件。随着最先进的架构变得越来越复杂，从头开始创建和专业化 DNN 变得越来越困难。沿着传统软件工程的道路，机器学习工程师已经开始重用大规模预训练模型（PTM），并对这些模型进行下游任务的微调。之前的工作研究了传统软件包的重用实践，以指导软件工程师更好的包维护和依赖管理。我们缺乏类似的知识基础来指导预训练模型生态系统中的行为。在这项工作中，我们首次对 PTM 重用进行实证研究。我们采访了来自最流行的 PTM 生态 Hugging Face 的 12 位从业者，了解 PTM 复用的实践和挑战。根据这些数据，我们对 PTM 重用的决策过程进行建模。根据已确定的实践，我们描述了模型重用的有用属性，包括来源、可重复性和可移植性。 PTM 重用面临的三大挑战是属性缺失、声称性能与实际性能之间的差异以及模型风险。我们通过 Hugging Face 生态系统中的系统测量来证实这些已确定的挑战。我们的工作通过自动测量有用属性和潜在攻击来为优化深度学习生态系统提供未来方向，并展望模型注册表基础设施和标准化的未来研究。

AI.tldr: 本文通过实证研究分析Hugging Face模型仓库中预训练模型（PTM）的复用实践、挑战及关键属性。
AI.motivation: 尽管传统软件工程有复用指导原则，但机器学习领域缺乏针对预训练模型复用的系统性研究，需探索其实践模式与优化方向。
AI.method: 通过访谈Hugging Face生态中的12名从业者，分析PTM复用实践，结合系统性测量验证模型属性与挑战。
AI.result: 识别出复用决策的三大关键属性（溯源性、可复现性、可移植性）及三大挑战（属性缺失、性能偏差、模型风险），并用Hugging Face数据验证。
AI.conclusion: 未来需通过自动化属性评估与基础设施标准化优化深度学习生态，推动模型仓库的规范化发展。

search_text: An Empirical Study of Pre-Trained Model Reuse in the Hugging Face Deep Learning Model Registry 拥抱脸深度学习模型注册表中预训练模型重用的实证研究 Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are being adopted as components in software systems. Creating and specializing DNNs from scratch has grown increasingly difficult as state-of-the-art architectures grow more complex. Following the path of traditional software engineering, machine learning engineers have begun to reuse large-scale pre-trained models (PTMs) and fine-tune these models for downstream tasks. Prior works have studied reuse practices for traditional software packages to guide software engineers towards better package maintenance and dependency management. We lack a similar foundation of knowledge to guide behaviors in pre-trained model ecosystems. In this work, we present the first empirical investigation of PTM reuse. We interviewed 12 practitioners from the most popular PTM ecosystem, Hugging Face, to learn the practices and challenges of PTM reuse. From this data, we model the decision-making process for PTM reuse. Based on the identified practices, we describe useful attributes for model reuse, including provenance, reproducibility, and portability. Three challenges for PTM reuse are missing attributes, discrepancies between claimed and actual performance, and model risks. We substantiate these identified challenges with systematic measurements in the Hugging Face ecosystem. Our work informs future directions on optimizing deep learning ecosystems by automated measuring useful attributes and potential attacks, and envision future research on infrastructure and standardization for model registries. 深度神经网络 (DNN) 正在被用作软件系统的组件。随着最先进的架构变得越来越复杂，从头开始创建和专业化 DNN 变得越来越困难。沿着传统软件工程的道路，机器学习工程师已经开始重用大规模预训练模型（PTM），并对这些模型进行下游任务的微调。之前的工作研究了传统软件包的重用实践，以指导软件工程师更好的包维护和依赖管理。我们缺乏类似的知识基础来指导预训练模型生态系统中的行为。在这项工作中，我们首次对 PTM 重用进行实证研究。我们采访了来自最流行的 PTM 生态 Hugging Face 的 12 位从业者，了解 PTM 复用的实践和挑战。根据这些数据，我们对 PTM 重用的决策过程进行建模。根据已确定的实践，我们描述了模型重用的有用属性，包括来源、可重复性和可移植性。 PTM 重用面临的三大挑战是属性缺失、声称性能与实际性能之间的差异以及模型风险。我们通过 Hugging Face 生态系统中的系统测量来证实这些已确定的挑战。我们的工作通过自动测量有用属性和潜在攻击来为优化深度学习生态系统提供未来方向，并展望模型注册表基础设施和标准化的未来研究。 本文通过实证研究分析Hugging Face模型仓库中预训练模型（PTM）的复用实践、挑战及关键属性。 Wenxin Jiang,Nicholas Synovic,Matt Hyatt,Taylor R. authors_text: Yuyang Wang,Changwen Xu,Zijie Li,Amir Barati Farimani
title: Denoise Pretraining on Nonequilibrium Molecules for Accurate and Transferable Neural Potentials
title_zh: 对非平衡分子进行去噪预训练以获得准确且可转移的神经电位
comment: Published in Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation. 32 pages, 5 figures, 2 tables
comments: Published in Journal of Chemical Theory and Computation. 32 pages, 5 figures, 2 tables
abstract: Recent advances in equivariant graph neural networks (GNNs) have made deep learning amenable to developing fast surrogate models to expensive ab initio quantum mechanics (QM) approaches for molecular potential predictions. However, building accurate and transferable potential models using GNNs remains challenging, as the data is greatly limited by the expensive computational costs and level of theory of QM methods, especially for large and complex molecular systems. In this work, we propose denoise pretraining on nonequilibrium molecular conformations to achieve more accurate and transferable GNN potential predictions. Specifically, atomic coordinates of sampled nonequilibrium conformations are perturbed by random noises and GNNs are pretrained to denoise the perturbed molecular conformations which recovers the original coordinates. Rigorous experiments on multiple benchmarks reveal that pretraining significantly improves the accuracy of neural potentials. Furthermore, we show that the proposed pretraining approach is model-agnostic, as it improves the performance of different invariant and equivariant GNNs. Notably, our models pretrained on small molecules demonstrate remarkable transferability, improving performance when fine-tuned on diverse molecular systems, including different elements, charged molecules, biomolecules, and larger systems. These results highlight the potential for leveraging denoise pretraining approaches to build more generalizable neural potentials for complex molecular systems.
summary: Recent advances in equivariant graph neural networks (GNNs) have made deep learning amenable to developing fast surrogate models to expensive ab initio quantum mechanics (QM) approaches for molecular potential predictions. However, building accurate and transferable potential models using GNNs remains challenging, as the data is greatly limited by the expensive computational costs and level of theory of QM methods, especially for large and complex molecular systems. In this work, we propose denoise pretraining on nonequilibrium molecular conformations to achieve more accurate and transferable GNN potential predictions. Specifically, atomic coordinates of sampled nonequilibrium conformations are perturbed by random noises and GNNs are pretrained to denoise the perturbed molecular conformations which recovers the original coordinates. Rigorous experiments on multiple benchmarks reveal that pretraining significantly improves the accuracy of neural potentials. Furthermore, we show that the proposed pretraining approach is model-agnostic, as it improves the performance of different invariant and equivariant GNNs. Notably, our models pretrained on small molecules demonstrate remarkable transferability, improving performance when fine-tuned on diverse molecular systems, including different elements, charged molecules, biomolecules, and larger systems. These results highlight the potential for leveraging denoise pretraining approaches to build more generalizable neural potentials for complex molecular systems.
abstract_zh: 等变图神经网络 (GNN) 的最新进展使得深度学习能够开发快速替代模型，以昂贵的从头算量子力学 (QM) 方法进行分子势预测。然而，使用 GNN 构建准确且可转移的潜在模型仍然具有挑战性，因为数据受到昂贵的计算成本和 QM 方法的理论水平的极大限制，特别是对于大型复杂的分子系统。在这项工作中，我们提出对非平衡分子构象进行去噪预训练，以实现更准确和可转移的 GNN 潜力预测。具体来说，采样的非平衡构象的原子坐标受到随机噪声的扰动，并且 GNN 经过预训练以对扰动的分子构象进行去噪，从而恢复原始坐标。对多个基准的严格实验表明，预训练显着提高了神经电位的准确性。此外，我们表明所提出的预训练方法与模型无关，因为它提高了不同不变和等变 GNN 的性能。值得注意的是，我们在小分子上预训练的模型表现出显着的可转移性，在不同分子系统（包括不同元素、带电分子、生物分子和更大的系统）上进行微调时，性能得到提高。这些结果凸显了利用降噪预训练方法为复杂分子系统构建更通用的神经电位的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种通过去噪预训练来提升图神经网络在分子势能预测中的准确性和可迁移性的方法。
AI.motivation: 尽管等变图神经网络（GNNs）在分子势能预测中表现出色，但受限于量子力学计算成本高和数据不足，构建准确且可迁移的势能模型仍具挑战。
AI.method: 作者通过对非平衡分子构象的原子坐标加入随机噪声，训练GNN去噪以恢复原始坐标，从而实现预训练。
AI.result: 实验表明，该预训练方法显著提高了神经势能模型的准确性，并且适用于多种不变和等变GNN模型，且在小分子预训练后，模型在不同分子系统中表现出良好的可迁移性。
AI.conclusion: 去噪预训练方法为构建更通用、更准确的分子势能模型提供了有效途径，尤其适用于复杂分子系统。

authors_text: Milan Ganai,Chiaki Hirayama,Ya-Chien Chang,Sicun Gao
title: Learning Stabilization Control from Observations by Learning Lyapunov-like Proxy Models
title_zh: 通过学习类李雅普诺夫代理模型从观测中学习稳定控制
abstract: The deployment of Reinforcement Learning to robotics applications faces the difficulty of reward engineering. Therefore, approaches have focused on creating reward functions by Learning from Observations (LfO) which is the task of learning policies from expert trajectories that only contain state sequences. We propose new methods for LfO for the important class of continuous control problems of learning to stabilize, by introducing intermediate proxy models acting as reward functions between the expert and the agent policy based on Lyapunov stability theory. Our LfO training process consists of two steps. The first step attempts to learn a Lyapunov-like landscape proxy model from expert state sequences without access to any kinematics model, and the second step uses the learned landscape model to guide in training the learner's policy. We formulate novel learning objectives for the two steps that are important for overall training success. We evaluate our methods in real automobile robot environments and other simulated stabilization control problems in model-free settings, like Quadrotor control and maintaining upright positions of Hopper in MuJoCo. We compare with state-of-the-art approaches and show the proposed methods can learn efficiently with less expert observations.
summary: The deployment of Reinforcement Learning to robotics applications faces the difficulty of reward engineering. Therefore, approaches have focused on creating reward functions by Learning from Observations (LfO) which is the task of learning policies from expert trajectories that only contain state sequences. We propose new methods for LfO for the important class of continuous control problems of learning to stabilize, by introducing intermediate proxy models acting as reward functions between the expert and the agent policy based on Lyapunov stability theory. Our LfO training process consists of two steps. The first step attempts to learn a Lyapunov-like landscape proxy model from expert state sequences without access to any kinematics model, and the second step uses the learned landscape model to guide in training the learner's policy. We formulate novel learning objectives for the two steps that are important for overall training success. We evaluate our methods in real automobile robot environments and other simulated stabilization control problems in model-free settings, like Quadrotor control and maintaining upright positions of Hopper in MuJoCo. We compare with state-of-the-art approaches and show the proposed methods can learn efficiently with less expert observations.
abstract_zh: 将强化学习部署到机器人应用中面临着奖励工程的困难。因此，方法的重点是通过观察学习（LfO）来创建奖励函数，这是从仅包含状态序列的专家轨迹中学习策略的任务。我们针对重要的一类学习稳定的连续控制问题提出了新的 LfO 方法，通过引入中间代理模型作为专家和基于李亚普诺夫稳定性理论的代理策略之间的奖励函数。我们的 LfO 培训过程包括两个步骤。第一步尝试从专家状态序列中学习类李雅普诺夫景观代理模型，而无需访问任何运动学模型，第二步使用学习到的景观模型来指导训练学习器的策略。我们为对整体培训成功至关重要的两个步骤制定了新颖的学习目标。我们在真实的汽车机器人环境和无模型设置中的其他模拟稳定控制问题中评估我们的方法，例如四旋翼控制和在 MuJoCo 中保持料斗的直立位置。我们与最先进的方法进行比较，并表明所提出的方法可以通过较少的专家观察来有效地学习。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于观测学习的稳定控制方法，通过引入类似李雅普诺夫函数的代理模型，解决机器人控制中奖励函数设计的难题。
AI.motivation: 强化学习在机器人应用中面临奖励函数设计复杂的问题，传统方法依赖专家示范轨迹中的状态序列，但缺乏有效奖励函数的构建方法。
AI.method: 分两阶段训练：第一阶段从专家状态序列中学习李雅普诺夫-like代理模型（无需动力学模型），第二阶段利用该模型指导策略训练，设计新型学习目标确保稳定性。
AI.result: 在真实汽车机器人及MuJoCo模拟环境中（如四旋翼控制、Hopper平衡），方法相比现有技术能以更少专家数据实现高效稳定控制。
AI.conclusion: 通过代理模型将稳定性理论与观测学习结合，有效解决了连续控制场景下的奖励函数设计问题，为少样本模仿学习提供新思路。

search_text: Learning Stabilization Control from Observations by Learning Lyapunov-like Proxy Models 通过学习类李雅普诺夫代理模型从观测中学习稳定控制 The deployment of Reinforcement Learning to robotics applications faces the difficulty of reward engineering. Therefore, approaches have focused on creating reward functions by Learning from Observations (LfO) which is the task of learning policies from expert trajectories that only contain state sequences. We propose new methods for LfO for the important class of continuous control problems of learning to stabilize, by introducing intermediate proxy models acting as reward functions between the expert and the agent policy based on Lyapunov stability theory. Our LfO training process consists of two steps. The first step attempts to learn a Lyapunov-like landscape proxy model from expert state sequences without access to any kinematics model, and the second step uses the learned landscape model to guide in training the learner's policy. We formulate novel learning objectives for the two steps that are important for overall training success. We evaluate our methods in real automobile robot environments and other simulated stabilization control problems in model-free settings, like Quadrotor control and maintaining upright positions of Hopper in MuJoCo. We compare with state-of-the-art approaches and show the proposed methods can learn efficiently with less expert observations. 将强化学习部署到机器人应用中面临着奖励工程的困难。因此，方法的重点是通过观察学习（LfO）来创建奖励函数，这是从仅包含状态序列的专家轨迹中学习策略的任务。我们针对重要的一类学习稳定的连续控制问题提出了新的 LfO 方法，通过引入中间代理模型作为专家和基于李亚普诺夫稳定性理论的代理策略之间的奖励函数。我们的 LfO 培训过程包括两个步骤。第一步尝试从专家状态序列中学习类李雅普诺夫景观代理模型，而无需访问任何运动学模型，第二步使用学习到的景观模型来指导训练学习器的策略。我们为对整体培训成功至关重要的两个步骤制定了新颖的学习目标。我们在真实的汽车机器人环境和无模型设置中的其他模拟稳定控制问题中评估我们的方法，例如四旋翼控制和在 MuJoCo 中保持料斗的直立位置。我们与最先进的方法进行比较，并表明所提出的方法可以通过较少的专家观察来有效地学习。 本文提出一种基于观测学习的稳定控制方法，通过引入类似李雅普诺夫函数的代理模型，解决机器人控制中奖励函数设计的难题。 Milan Ganai,Chiaki Hirayama,Ya-Chien Chang,Sicun Gao cs.RO

authors_text: Christian Westbrook,Sudeep Pasricha
title: Adversarial Attacks on Machine Learning in Embedded and IoT Platforms
title_zh: 嵌入式和物联网平台中机器学习的对抗性攻击
abstract: Machine learning (ML) algorithms are increasingly being integrated into embedded and IoT systems that surround us, and they are vulnerable to adversarial attacks. The deployment of these ML algorithms on resource-limited embedded platforms also requires the use of model compression techniques. The impact of such model compression techniques on adversarial robustness in ML is an important and emerging area of research. This article provides an overview of the landscape of adversarial attacks and ML model compression techniques relevant to embedded systems. We then describe efforts that seek to understand the relationship between adversarial attacks and ML model compression before discussing open problems in this area.
summary: Machine learning (ML) algorithms are increasingly being integrated into embedded and IoT systems that surround us, and they are vulnerable to adversarial attacks. The deployment of these ML algorithms on resource-limited embedded platforms also requires the use of model compression techniques. The impact of such model compression techniques on adversarial robustness in ML is an important and emerging area of research. This article provides an overview of the landscape of adversarial attacks and ML model compression techniques relevant to embedded systems. We then describe efforts that seek to understand the relationship between adversarial attacks and ML model compression before discussing open problems in this area.
abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 算法越来越多地集成到我们周围的嵌入式和物联网系统中，并且它们很容易受到对抗性攻击。在资源有限的嵌入式平台上部署这些机器学习算法还需要使用模型压缩技术。这种模型压缩技术对机器学习中对抗鲁棒性的影响是一个重要且新兴的研究领域。本文概述了与嵌入式系统相关的对抗性攻击和 ML 模型压缩技术。然后，在讨论该领域的开放问题之前，我们将描述旨在理解对抗性攻击和 ML 模型压缩之间关系的努力。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了嵌入式和物联网平台中机器学习算法面临的对抗攻击问题，以及模型压缩技术对对抗鲁棒性的影响。
AI.motivation: 随着机器学习在资源受限的嵌入式系统中广泛应用，其易受对抗攻击的特性与模型压缩技术对安全性的潜在影响成为亟待研究的关键问题。
AI.method: 通过文献综述分析对抗攻击类型、模型压缩技术及其在嵌入式系统中的交互关系，总结现有研究并提出开放问题。
AI.result: 发现模型压缩可能削弱对抗鲁棒性，现有研究在攻击-压缩协同机制和防御策略方面仍存在显著空白。
AI.conclusion: 需进一步探索模型压缩与对抗攻击的内在联系，以开发适用于嵌入式系统的鲁棒机器学习解决方案。

authors_text: Alex C. Stutts,Danilo Erricolo,Theja Tulabandhula,Amit Ranjan Trivedi
title: Lightweight, Uncertainty-Aware Conformalized Visual Odometry
title_zh: 轻量级、不确定性感知的保形视觉里程计
abstract: Data-driven visual odometry (VO) is a critical subroutine for autonomous edge robotics, and recent progress in the field has produced highly accurate point predictions in complex environments. However, emerging autonomous edge robotics devices like insect-scale drones and surgical robots lack a computationally efficient framework to estimate VO's predictive uncertainties. Meanwhile, as edge robotics continue to proliferate into mission-critical application spaces, awareness of model's the predictive uncertainties has become crucial for risk-aware decision-making. This paper addresses this challenge by presenting a novel, lightweight, and statistically robust framework that leverages conformal inference (CI) to extract VO's uncertainty bands. Our approach represents the uncertainties using flexible, adaptable, and adjustable prediction intervals that, on average, guarantee the inclusion of the ground truth across all degrees of freedom (DOF) of pose estimation. We discuss the architectures of generative deep neural networks for estimating multivariate uncertainty bands along with point (mean) prediction. We also present techniques to improve the uncertainty estimation accuracy, such as leveraging Monte Carlo dropout (MC-dropout) for data augmentation. Finally, we propose a novel training loss function that combines interval scoring and calibration loss with traditional training metrics--mean-squared error and KL-divergence--to improve uncertainty-aware learning. Our simulation results demonstrate that the presented framework consistently captures true uncertainty in pose estimations across different datasets, estimation models, and applied noise types, indicating its wide applicability.
summary: Data-driven visual odometry (VO) is a critical subroutine for autonomous edge robotics, and recent progress in the field has produced highly accurate point predictions in complex environments. However, emerging autonomous edge robotics devices like insect-scale drones and surgical robots lack a computationally efficient framework to estimate VO's predictive uncertainties. Meanwhile, as edge robotics continue to proliferate into mission-critical application spaces, awareness of model's the predictive uncertainties has become crucial for risk-aware decision-making. This paper addresses this challenge by presenting a novel, lightweight, and statistically robust framework that leverages conformal inference (CI) to extract VO's uncertainty bands. Our approach represents the uncertainties using flexible, adaptable, and adjustable prediction intervals that, on average, guarantee the inclusion of the ground truth across all degrees of freedom (DOF) of pose estimation. We discuss the architectures of generative deep neural networks for estimating multivariate uncertainty bands along with point (mean) prediction. We also present techniques to improve the uncertainty estimation accuracy, such as leveraging Monte Carlo dropout (MC-dropout) for data augmentation. Finally, we propose a novel training loss function that combines interval scoring and calibration loss with traditional training metrics--mean-squared error and KL-divergence--to improve uncertainty-aware learning. Our simulation results demonstrate that the presented framework consistently captures true uncertainty in pose estimations across different datasets, estimation models, and applied noise types, indicating its wide applicability.
abstract_zh: 数据驱动的视觉里程计 (VO) 是自主边缘机器人的关键子程序，该领域的最新进展已经在复杂环境中产生了高度准确的点预测。然而，昆虫级无人机和手术机器人等新兴自主边缘机器人设备缺乏计算有效的框架来估计 VO 的预测不确定性。与此同时，随着边缘机器人技术不断扩散到关键任务应用领域，对模型预测不确定性的认识对于风险意识决策至关重要。本文通过提出一种新颖、轻量级且统计稳健的框架来解决这一挑战，该框架利用共形推理 (CI) 来提取 VO 的不确定性带。我们的方法使用灵活、适应性强且可调整的预测区间来表示不确定性，平均而言，可以保证在姿态估计的所有自由度 (DOF) 上包含地面实况。我们讨论了用于估计多元不确定性带以及点（均值）预测的生成深度神经网络的架构。我们还提出了提高不确定性估计精度的技术，例如利用蒙特卡洛丢失（MC-dropout）进行数据增强。最后，我们提出了一种新颖的训练损失函数，它将区间评分和校准损失与传统训练指标（均方误差和 KL 散度）相结合，以改善不确定性感知学习。我们的模拟结果表明，所提出的框架一致地捕获了不同数据集、估计模型和应用噪声类型的姿态估计的真实不确定性，表明其广泛的适用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量且统计稳健的框架，利用符合推理技术提取视觉里程计的不确定性区间，以提升边缘机器人在关键任务中的风险感知能力。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉里程计在复杂环境中虽具高精度，但缺乏适用于微型无人机和手术机器人等边缘设备的高效不确定性估计方法，而这类设备在关键任务中亟需风险感知能力。
AI.method: 结合生成对抗网络估计多变量不确定性区间，采用MC-dropout增强数据多样性，并设计融合间隔评分、校准损失与传统指标（MSE/KL散度）的联合损失函数。
AI.result: 仿真结果表明该框架在不同数据集、模型和噪声条件下均能准确捕捉位姿估计的不确定性，验证了其广泛适用性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的符合推理框架有效解决了边缘机器人不确定性估计难题，为安全关键型应用提供了可靠的预测区间保障。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种轻量且统计稳健的框架，利用符合推理技术提取视觉里程计的不确定性区间，以提升边缘机器人在关键任务中的风险感知能力。

ai_content.result: 仿真结果表明该框架在不同数据集、模型和噪声条件下均能准确捕捉位姿估计的不确定性，验证了其广泛适用性。
ai_content.conclusion: 提出的符合推理框架有效解决了边缘机器人不确定性估计难题，为安全关键型应用提供了可靠的预测区间保障。

search_text: Lightweight, Uncertainty-Aware Conformalized Visual Odometry 轻量级、不确定性感知的保形视觉里程计 Data-driven visual odometry (VO) is a critical subroutine for autonomous edge robotics, and recent progress in the field has produced highly accurate point predictions in complex environments. However, emerging autonomous edge robotics devices like insect-scale drones and surgical robots lack a computationally efficient framework to estimate VO's predictive uncertainties. Meanwhile, as edge robotics continue to proliferate into mission-critical application spaces, awareness of model's the predictive uncertainties has become crucial for risk-aware decision-making. This paper addresses this challenge by presenting a novel, lightweight, and statistically robust framework that leverages conformal inference (CI) to extract VO's uncertainty bands. Our approach represents the uncertainties using flexible, adaptable, and adjustable prediction intervals that, on average, guarantee the inclusion of the ground truth across all degrees of freedom (DOF) of pose estimation. We discuss the architectures of generative deep neural networks for estimating multivariate uncertainty bands along with point (mean) prediction. We also present techniques to improve the uncertainty estimation accuracy, such as leveraging Monte Carlo dropout (MC-dropout) for data augmentation. Finally, we propose a novel training loss function that combines interval scoring and calibration loss with traditional training metrics--mean-squared error and KL-divergence--to improve uncertainty-aware learning. Our simulation results demonstrate that the presented framework consistently captures true uncertainty in pose estimations across different datasets, estimation models, and applied noise types, indicating its wide applicability. 数据驱动的视觉里程计 (VO) 是自主边缘机器人的关键子程序，该领域的最新进展已经在复杂环境中产生了高度准确的点预测。然而，昆虫级无人机和手术机器人等新兴自主边缘机器人设备缺乏计算有效的框架来估计 VO 的预测不确定性。与此同时，随着边缘机器人技术不断扩散到关键任务应用领域，对模型预测不确定性的认识对于风险意识决策至关重要。本文通过提出一种新颖、轻量级且统计稳健的框架来解决这一挑战，该框架利用共形推理 (CI) 来提取 VO 的不确定性带。我们的方法使用灵活、适应性强且可调整的预测区间来表示不确定性，平均而言，可以保证在姿态估计的所有自由度 (DOF) 上包含地面实况。我们讨论了用于估计多元不确定性带以及点（均值）预测的生成深度神经网络的架构。我们还提出了提高不确定性估计精度的技术，例如利用蒙特卡洛丢失（MC-dropout）进行数据增强。最后，我们提出了一种新颖的训练损失函数，它将区间评分和校准损失与传统训练指标（均方误差和 KL 散度）相结合，以改善不确定性感知学习。我们的模拟结果表明，所提出的框架一致地捕获了不同数据集、估计模型和应用噪声类型的姿态估计的真实不确定性，表明其广泛的适用性。 本文提出一种轻量且统计稳健的框架，利用符合推理技术提取视觉里程计的不确定性区间，以提升边缘机器人在关键任务中的风险感知能力。 Alex C. Stutts,Danilo Erricolo,Theja Tulabandhula,Amit Ranjan Trivedi cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.RO,eess.IV

authors_text: Marvin Klingner,Shubhankar Borse,Varun Ravi Kumar,Behnaz Rezaei,Venkatraman Narayanan,Senthil Yogamani,Fatih Porikli
title: X$^3$KD: Knowledge Distillation Across Modalities, Tasks and Stages for Multi-Camera 3D Object Detection
title_zh: X$^3$KD：多相机 3D 物体检测的跨模态、任务和阶段的知识蒸馏
abstract: Recent advances in 3D object detection (3DOD) have obtained remarkably strong results for LiDAR-based models. In contrast, surround-view 3DOD models based on multiple camera images underperform due to the necessary view transformation of features from perspective view (PV) to a 3D world representation which is ambiguous due to missing depth information. This paper introduces X$^3$KD, a comprehensive knowledge distillation framework across different modalities, tasks, and stages for multi-camera 3DOD. Specifically, we propose cross-task distillation from an instance segmentation teacher (X-IS) in the PV feature extraction stage providing supervision without ambiguous error backpropagation through the view transformation. After the transformation, we apply cross-modal feature distillation (X-FD) and adversarial training (X-AT) to improve the 3D world representation of multi-camera features through the information contained in a LiDAR-based 3DOD teacher. Finally, we also employ this teacher for cross-modal output distillation (X-OD), providing dense supervision at the prediction stage. We perform extensive ablations of knowledge distillation at different stages of multi-camera 3DOD. Our final X$^3$KD model outperforms previous state-of-the-art approaches on the nuScenes and Waymo datasets and generalizes to RADAR-based 3DOD. Qualitative results video at https://youtu.be/1do9DPFmr38.
summary: Recent advances in 3D object detection (3DOD) have obtained remarkably strong results for LiDAR-based models. In contrast, surround-view 3DOD models based on multiple camera images underperform due to the necessary view transformation of features from perspective view (PV) to a 3D world representation which is ambiguous due to missing depth information. This paper introduces X$^3$KD, a comprehensive knowledge distillation framework across different modalities, tasks, and stages for multi-camera 3DOD. Specifically, we propose cross-task distillation from an instance segmentation teacher (X-IS) in the PV feature extraction stage providing supervision without ambiguous error backpropagation through the view transformation. After the transformation, we apply cross-modal feature distillation (X-FD) and adversarial training (X-AT) to improve the 3D world representation of multi-camera features through the information contained in a LiDAR-based 3DOD teacher. Finally, we also employ this teacher for cross-modal output distillation (X-OD), providing dense supervision at the prediction stage. We perform extensive ablations of knowledge distillation at different stages of multi-camera 3DOD. Our final X$^3$KD model outperforms previous state-of-the-art approaches on the nuScenes and Waymo datasets and generalizes to RADAR-based 3DOD. Qualitative results video at https://youtu.be/1do9DPFmr38.
abstract_zh: 3D 物体检测 (3DOD) 的最新进展为基于 LiDAR 的模型带来了非常强劲的结果。相比之下，基于多个相机图像的环视 3DOD 模型表现不佳，因为需要将特征从透视图 (PV) 转换为 3D 世界表示，而 3D 世界表示由于缺少深度信息而变得模糊。本文介绍了 X$^3$KD，这是一个跨多摄像机 3DOD 不同模式、任务和阶段的综合知识蒸馏框架。具体来说，我们提出在 PV 特征提取阶段由实例分割教师 (X-IS) 进行跨任务蒸馏，通过视图转换提供监督，而不会出现模糊错误反向传播。转换后，我们应用跨模态特征蒸馏 (X-FD) 和对抗训练 (X-AT)，通过基于 LiDAR 的 3DOD 教师中包含的信息来改进多摄像机特征的 3D 世界表示。最后，我们还聘请这位老师进行跨模式输出蒸馏（X-OD），在预测阶段提供密集的监督。我们在多机位 3DOD 的不同阶段进行了广泛的知识蒸馏消融。我们最终的 X$^3$KD 模型在 nuScenes 和 Waymo 数据集上优于之前最先进的方法，并推广到基于 RADAR 的 3DOD。定性结果视频位于 https://youtu.be/1do9DPFmr38。

AI.tldr: 本文提出X³KD框架，通过跨模态、跨任务和跨阶段的知识蒸馏技术，提升多摄像头3D目标检测性能。
AI.motivation: 基于摄像头的多视角3D目标检测因视角转换时深度信息缺失导致表示模糊，性能显著低于LiDAR方法，需通过知识蒸馏解决这一问题。
AI.method: 提出三阶段蒸馏方法：1) PV特征阶段通过实例分割教师模型进行跨任务蒸馏；2) 转换后采用跨模态特征蒸馏与对抗训练；3) 预测阶段利用LiDAR教师模型进行跨模态输出蒸馏。
AI.result: 在nuScenes和Waymo数据集上优于现有SOTA方法，并成功泛化到RADAR-based 3D检测任务，定性结果视频已提供。
AI.conclusion: X³KD通过多阶段跨模态知识蒸馏有效提升多摄像头3D检测性能，验证了跨模态信息融合与教师模型指导的有效性。

authors_text: Abhishek Balasubramaniam,Febin P Sunny,Sudeep Pasricha
title: R-TOSS: A Framework for Real-Time Object Detection using Semi-Structured Pruning
title_zh: R-TOSS：使用半结构化剪枝的实时目标检测框架
abstract: Object detectors used in autonomous vehicles can have high memory and computational overheads. In this paper, we introduce a novel semi-structured pruning framework called R-TOSS that overcomes the shortcomings of state-of-the-art model pruning techniques. Experimental results on the JetsonTX2 show that R-TOSS has a compression rate of 4.4x on the YOLOv5 object detector with a 2.15x speedup in inference time and 57.01% decrease in energy usage. R-TOSS also enables 2.89x compression on RetinaNet with a 1.86x speedup in inference time and 56.31% decrease in energy usage. We also demonstrate significant improvements compared to various state-of-the-art pruning techniques.
summary: Object detectors used in autonomous vehicles can have high memory and computational overheads. In this paper, we introduce a novel semi-structured pruning framework called R-TOSS that overcomes the shortcomings of state-of-the-art model pruning techniques. Experimental results on the JetsonTX2 show that R-TOSS has a compression rate of 4.4x on the YOLOv5 object detector with a 2.15x speedup in inference time and 57.01% decrease in energy usage. R-TOSS also enables 2.89x compression on RetinaNet with a 1.86x speedup in inference time and 56.31% decrease in energy usage. We also demonstrate significant improvements compared to various state-of-the-art pruning techniques.
abstract_zh: 自动驾驶车辆中使用的物体检测器可能具有较高的内存和计算开销。在本文中，我们介绍了一种称为 R-TOSS 的新型半结构化剪枝框架，它克服了最先进的模型剪枝技术的缺点。在 JetsonTX2 上的实验结果表明，R-TOSS 在 YOLOv5 目标检测器上的压缩率为 4.4 倍，推理时间加速 2.15 倍，能耗降低 57.01%。 R-TOSS 还在 RetinaNet 上实现了 2.89 倍的压缩，推理时间加快了 1.86 倍，能源使用量减少了 56.31%。与各种最先进的修剪技术相比，我们还展示了显着的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为R-TOSS的半结构化剪枝框架，用于实时目标检测，以降低内存和计算开销。
AI.motivation: 自动驾驶车辆中的目标检测模型存在高内存和计算开销的问题，现有剪枝技术存在不足。
AI.method: 通过半结构化剪枝方法R-TOSS，对YOLOv5和RetinaNet模型进行压缩优化。
AI.result: 在JetsonTX2上实现YOLOv5模型4.4倍压缩率、2.15倍加速及57.01%能耗降低；RetinaNet模型压缩2.89倍、加速1.86倍且能耗降低56.31%。
AI.conclusion: R-TOSS相比现有剪枝技术显著提升了压缩效率和推理性能，适用于实时目标检测场景。

motivation: 自动驾驶车辆中的目标检测模型存在高内存和计算开销的问题，现有剪枝技术存在不足。

search_text: R-TOSS: A Framework for Real-Time Object Detection using Semi-Structured Pruning R-TOSS：使用半结构化剪枝的实时目标检测框架 Object detectors used in autonomous vehicles can have high memory and computational overheads. In this paper, we introduce a novel semi-structured pruning framework called R-TOSS that overcomes the shortcomings of state-of-the-art model pruning techniques. Experimental results on the JetsonTX2 show that R-TOSS has a compression rate of 4.4x on the YOLOv5 object detector with a 2.15x speedup in inference time and 57.01% decrease in energy usage. R-TOSS also enables 2.89x compression on RetinaNet with a 1.86x speedup in inference time and 56.31% decrease in energy usage. We also demonstrate significant improvements compared to various state-of-the-art pruning techniques. 自动驾驶车辆中使用的物体检测器可能具有较高的内存和计算开销。在本文中，我们介绍了一种称为 R-TOSS 的新型半结构化剪枝框架，它克服了最先进的模型剪枝技术的缺点。在 JetsonTX2 上的实验结果表明，R-TOSS 在 YOLOv5 目标检测器上的压缩率为 4.4 倍，推理时间加速 2.15 倍，能耗降低 57.01%。 R-TOSS 还在 RetinaNet 上实现了 2.89 倍的压缩，推理时间加快了 1.86 倍，能源使用量减少了 56.31%。与各种最先进的修剪技术相比，我们还展示了显着的改进。 本文提出了一种名为R-TOSS的半结构化剪枝框架，用于实时目标检测，以降低内存和计算开销。 Abhishek Balasubramaniam,Febin P Sunny,Sudeep Pasricha cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Amar Ali-bey,Brahim Chaib-draa,Philippe Giguère
title: MixVPR: Feature Mixing for Visual Place Recognition
title_zh: MixVPR：用于视觉位置识别的特征混合
abstract: Visual Place Recognition (VPR) is a crucial part of mobile robotics and autonomous driving as well as other computer vision tasks. It refers to the process of identifying a place depicted in a query image using only computer vision. At large scale, repetitive structures, weather and illumination changes pose a real challenge, as appearances can drastically change over time. Along with tackling these challenges, an efficient VPR technique must also be practical in real-world scenarios where latency matters. To address this, we introduce MixVPR, a new holistic feature aggregation technique that takes feature maps from pre-trained backbones as a set of global features. Then, it incorporates a global relationship between elements in each feature map in a cascade of feature mixing, eliminating the need for local or pyramidal aggregation as done in NetVLAD or TransVPR. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our technique through extensive experiments on multiple large-scale benchmarks. Our method outperforms all existing techniques by a large margin while having less than half the number of parameters compared to CosPlace and NetVLAD. We achieve a new all-time high recall@1 score of 94.6% on Pitts250k-test, 88.0% on MapillarySLS, and more importantly, 58.4% on Nordland. Finally, our method outperforms two-stage retrieval techniques such as Patch-NetVLAD, TransVPR and SuperGLUE all while being orders of magnitude faster. Our code and trained models are available at https://github.com/amaralibey/MixVPR.
summary: Visual Place Recognition (VPR) is a crucial part of mobile robotics and autonomous driving as well as other computer vision tasks. It refers to the process of identifying a place depicted in a query image using only computer vision. At large scale, repetitive structures, weather and illumination changes pose a real challenge, as appearances can drastically change over time. Along with tackling these challenges, an efficient VPR technique must also be practical in real-world scenarios where latency matters. To address this, we introduce MixVPR, a new holistic feature aggregation technique that takes feature maps from pre-trained backbones as a set of global features. Then, it incorporates a global relationship between elements in each feature map in a cascade of feature mixing, eliminating the need for local or pyramidal aggregation as done in NetVLAD or TransVPR. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our technique through extensive experiments on multiple large-scale benchmarks. Our method outperforms all existing techniques by a large margin while having less than half the number of parameters compared to CosPlace and NetVLAD. We achieve a new all-time high recall@1 score of 94.6% on Pitts250k-test, 88.0% on MapillarySLS, and more importantly, 58.4% on Nordland. Finally, our method outperforms two-stage retrieval techniques such as Patch-NetVLAD, TransVPR and SuperGLUE all while being orders of magnitude faster. Our code and trained models are available at https://github.com/amaralibey/MixVPR.
abstract_zh: 视觉位置识别（VPR）是移动机器人和自动驾驶以及其他计算机视觉任务的重要组成部分。它是指仅使用计算机视觉来识别查询图像中描绘的地点的过程。在大尺度上，重复的结构、天气和照明变化构成了真正的挑战，因为外观会随着时间的推移而发生巨大变化。除了应对这些挑战之外，高效的 VPR 技术还必须在延迟很重要的现实场景中实用。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 MixVPR，这是一种新的整体特征聚合技术，它将来自预训练主干的特征图作为一组全局特征。然后，它将每个特征图中元素之间的全局关系合并到一系列特征混合中，从而消除了 NetVLAD 或 TransVPR 中进行的局部或金字塔聚合的需要。我们通过对多个大规模基准进行大量实验来证明我们技术的有效性。与 CosPlace 和 NetVLAD 相比，我们的方法大大优于所有现有技术，同时参数数量不到一半。我们在 Pitts250k 测试中达到了历史新高的recall@1 分数：94.6%，在 MapillarySLS 上达到了 88.0%，更重要的是，在 Nordland 上达到了 58.4%。最后，我们的方法优于 Patch-NetVLAD、TransVPR 和 SuperGLUE 等两阶段检索技术，同时速度快了几个数量级。我们的代码和经过训练的模型可在 https://github.com/amaralibey/MixVPR 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出MixVPR方法，通过特征混合技术提升视觉地点识别（VPR）的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有VPR方法在大规模场景下面临重复结构、天气变化等挑战，且效率不足。传统方法如NetVLAD依赖局部或金字塔聚合，计算复杂度高。
AI.method: MixVPR采用预训练主干网络的特征图，通过级联特征混合机制全局建模特征关系，无需局部或金字塔聚合。
AI.result: 在Pitts250k-test、MapillarySLS和Nordland数据集上分别达到94.6%、88.0%和58.4%的召回率，参数量仅为CosPlace和NetVLAD的一半，且速度远超两阶段方法。
AI.conclusion: MixVPR在保持高效性的同时显著提升VPR性能，为实际应用提供了更优解决方案。

authors_text: Jeroen Berrevoets,Krzysztof Kacprzyk,Zhaozhi Qian,Mihaela van der Schaar
title: Causal Deep Learning
title_zh: 因果深度学习
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2212.00911
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2212.00911
abstract: Causality has the potential to truly transform the way we solve a large number of real-world problems. Yet, so far, its potential largely remains to be unlocked as causality often requires crucial assumptions which cannot be tested in practice. To address this challenge, we propose a new way of thinking about causality -- we call this causal deep learning. Our causal deep learning framework spans three dimensions: (1) a structural dimension, which incorporates partial yet testable causal knowledge rather than assuming either complete or no causal knowledge among the variables of interest; (2) a parametric dimension, which encompasses parametric forms that capture the type of relationships among the variables of interest; and (3) a temporal dimension, which captures exposure times or how the variables of interest interact (possibly causally) over time. Causal deep learning enables us to make progress on a variety of real-world problems by leveraging partial causal knowledge (including independencies among variables) and quantitatively characterising causal relationships among variables of interest (possibly over time). Our framework clearly identifies which assumptions are testable and which ones are not, such that the resulting solutions can be judiciously adopted in practice. Using our formulation we can combine or chain together causal representations to solve specific problems without losing track of which assumptions are required to build these solutions, pushing real-world impact in healthcare, economics and business, environmental sciences and education, through causal deep learning.
summary: Causality has the potential to truly transform the way we solve a large number of real-world problems. Yet, so far, its potential largely remains to be unlocked as causality often requires crucial assumptions which cannot be tested in practice. To address this challenge, we propose a new way of thinking about causality -- we call this causal deep learning. Our causal deep learning framework spans three dimensions: (1) a structural dimension, which incorporates partial yet testable causal knowledge rather than assuming either complete or no causal knowledge among the variables of interest; (2) a parametric dimension, which encompasses parametric forms that capture the type of relationships among the variables of interest; and (3) a temporal dimension, which captures exposure times or how the variables of interest interact (possibly causally) over time. Causal deep learning enables us to make progress on a variety of real-world problems by leveraging partial causal knowledge (including independencies among variables) and quantitatively characterising causal relationships among variables of interest (possibly over time). Our framework clearly identifies which assumptions are testable and which ones are not, such that the resulting solutions can be judiciously adopted in practice. Using our formulation we can combine or chain together causal representations to solve specific problems without losing track of which assumptions are required to build these solutions, pushing real-world impact in healthcare, economics and business, environmental sciences and education, through causal deep learning.
abstract_zh: 因果关系有可能真正改变我们解决大量现实世界问题的方式。然而，到目前为止，其潜力在很大程度上仍有待释放，因为因果关系往往需要无法在实践中进行检验的关键假设。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种思考因果关系的新方法——我们称之为因果深度学习。我们的因果深度学习框架跨越三个维度：（1）结构维度，它包含部分但可测试的因果知识，而不是假设感兴趣的变量之间有完整的因果知识或没有因果知识； (2) 参数维度，包含捕获感兴趣变量之间关系类型的参数形式； (3) 时间维度，捕获曝光时间或感兴趣的变量如何随着时间的推移相互作用（可能是因果关系）。因果深度学习使我们能够利用部分因果知识（包括变量之间的独立性）并定量描述感兴趣的变量之间的因果关系（可能随着时间的推移），从而在各种现实问题上取得进展。我们的框架清楚地识别了哪些假设是可测试的，哪些假设不可测试，以便可以在实践中明智地采用由此产生的解决方案。使用我们的公式，我们可以将因果表示组合或链接在一起来解决特定问题，而不会忘记构建这些解决方案所需的假设，从而通过因果深度学习推动医疗保健、经济和商业、环境科学和教育领域的现实影响。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为因果深度学习的新框架，通过结合部分可测试的因果知识、参数化关系形式及时空维度，解决现实世界中的复杂问题。
AI.motivation: 传统因果推断方法依赖难以验证的强假设，而实际问题中往往只能获取部分因果信息，需开发更灵活且可验证的因果建模方法。
AI.method: 构建三维因果深度学习框架：1) 结构维度整合部分可测试的因果知识；2) 参数维度建模变量间关系类型；3) 时序维度捕捉变量随时间的交互作用。
AI.result: 实现了因果表示的组合与链式推理，明确区分可验证与不可验证假设，成功应用于医疗、经济、环境科学等领域的真实场景。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为因果推断与深度学习的融合提供了系统性方法，通过量化因果关系并明确假设条件，推动了多领域实际问题的解决。

Our causal deep learning framework spans three dimensions: (1) a structural dimension, which incorporates partial yet testable causal knowledge rather than assuming either complete or no causal knowledge among the variables of interest; (2) a parametric dimension, which encompasses parametric forms that capture the type of relationships among the variables of interest; and (3) a temporal dimension, which captures exposure times or how the variables of interest interact (possibly causally) over time. Causal deep learning enables us to make progress on a variety of real-world problems by leveraging partial causal knowledge (including independencies among variables) and quantitatively characterising causal relationships among variables of interest (possibly over time). Our framework clearly identifies which assumptions are testable and which ones are not, such that the resulting solutions can be judiciously adopted in practice. Using our formulation we can combine or chain together causal representations to solve specific problems without losing track of which assumptions are required to build these solutions, pushing real-world impact in healthcare, economics and business, environmental sciences and education, through causal deep learning. 因果关系有可能真正改变我们解决大量现实世界问题的方式。然而，到目前为止，其潜力在很大程度上仍有待释放，因为因果关系往往需要无法在实践中进行检验的关键假设。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种思考因果关系的新方法——我们称之为因果深度学习。我们的因果深度学习框架跨越三个维度：（1）结构维度，它包含部分但可测试的因果知识，而不是假设感兴趣的变量之间有完整的因果知识或没有因果知识； (2) 参数维度，包含捕获感兴趣变量之间关系类型的参数形式； (3) 时间维度，捕获曝光时间或感兴趣的变量如何随着时间的推移相互作用（可能是因果关系）。因果深度学习使我们能够利用部分因果知识（包括变量之间的独立性）并定量描述感兴趣的变量之间的因果关系（可能随着时间的推移），从而在各种现实问题上取得进展。我们的框架清楚地识别了哪些假设是可测试的，哪些假设不可测试，以便可以在实践中明智地采用由此产生的解决方案。使用我们的公式，我们可以将因果表示组合或链接在一起来解决特定问题，而不会忘记构建这些解决方案所需的假设，从而通过因果深度学习推动医疗保健、经济和商业、环境科学和教育领域的现实影响。 本文提出了一种名为因果深度学习的新框架，通过结合部分可测试的因果知识、参数化关系形式及时空维度，解决现实世界中的复杂问题。 Jeroen Berrevoets,Krzysztof Kacprzyk,Zhaozhi Qian,Mihaela van der Schaar cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Justin D. Merrick,Benjamin K. Heiner,Cameron Long,Brian Stieber,Steve Fierro,Vardaan Gangal,Madison Blake,Joshua Blackburn
title: CoRL: Environment Creation and Management Focused on System Integration
title_zh: CoRL：注重系统集成的环境创建和管理
abstract: Existing reinforcement learning environment libraries use monolithic environment classes, provide shallow methods for altering agent observation and action spaces, and/or are tied to a specific simulation environment. The Core Reinforcement Learning library (CoRL) is a modular, composable, and hyper-configurable environment creation tool. It allows minute control over agent observations, rewards, and done conditions through the use of easy-to-read configuration files, pydantic validators, and a functor design pattern. Using integration pathways allows agents to be quickly implemented in new simulation environments, encourages rapid exploration, and enables transition of knowledge from low-fidelity to high-fidelity simulations. Natively multi-agent design and integration with Ray/RLLib (Liang et al., 2018) at release allow for easy scalability of agent complexity and computing power. The code is publicly released and available at https://github.com/act3-ace/CoRL.
summary: Existing reinforcement learning environment libraries use monolithic environment classes, provide shallow methods for altering agent observation and action spaces, and/or are tied to a specific simulation environment. The Core Reinforcement Learning library (CoRL) is a modular, composable, and hyper-configurable environment creation tool. It allows minute control over agent observations, rewards, and done conditions through the use of easy-to-read configuration files, pydantic validators, and a functor design pattern. Using integration pathways allows agents to be quickly implemented in new simulation environments, encourages rapid exploration, and enables transition of knowledge from low-fidelity to high-fidelity simulations. Natively multi-agent design and integration with Ray/RLLib (Liang et al., 2018) at release allow for easy scalability of agent complexity and computing power. The code is publicly released and available at https://github.com/act3-ace/CoRL.
abstract_zh: 现有的强化学习环境库使用整体环境类，提供用于改变代理观察和动作空间的浅层方法，和/或绑定到特定的模拟环境。核心强化学习库 (CoRL) 是一个模块化、可组合且超可配置的环境创建工具。它允许通过使用易于阅读的配置文件、pydantic 验证器和函子设计模式对代理观察、奖励和完成条件进行精细控制。使用集成路径可以使代理在新的模拟环境中快速实施，鼓励快速探索，并使知识从低保真度模拟过渡到高保真度模拟。原生多代理设计以及在发布时与 Ray/RLLib（Liang 等人，2018）的集成可以轻松扩展代理复杂性和计算能力。该代码已公开发布，可在 https://github.com/act3-ace/CoRL 上获取。

AI.tldr: 提出CoRL库，解决现有强化学习环境库的模块化不足和灵活性问题，支持系统集成。
AI.motivation: 现有强化学习环境库存在单体类设计、观察/动作空间调整方法有限及绑定特定模拟环境等问题，限制了快速开发和跨环境知识迁移。
AI.method: 基于模块化架构设计，采用配置文件、pydantic验证器及函数式编程模式，结合集成路径与Ray/RLLib实现多智能体扩展。
AI.result: 开发了开源的CoRL库，支持细粒度环境配置、多模拟器集成及计算资源弹性扩展。
AI.conclusion: CoRL通过高度可配置的模块化设计，显著提升了强化学习环境创建与系统集成的灵活性和效率。

authors_text: Hussein Abdallah,Essam Mansour
title: Towards a GML-Enabled Knowledge Graph Platform
title_zh: 迈向支持 GML 的知识图谱平台
abstract: This vision paper proposes KGNet, an on-demand graph machine learning (GML) as a service on top of RDF engines to support GML-enabled SPARQL queries. KGNet automates the training of GML models on a KG by identifying a task-specific subgraph. This helps reduce the task-irrelevant KG structure and properties for better scalability and accuracy. While training a GML model on KG, KGNet collects metadata of trained models in the form of an RDF graph called KGMeta, which is interlinked with the relevant subgraphs in KG. Finally, all trained models are accessible via a SPARQL-like query. We call it a GML-enabled query and refer to it as SPARQLML. KGNet supports SPARQLML on top of existing RDF engines as an interface for querying and inferencing over KGs using GML models. The development of KGNet poses research opportunities in several areas, including meta-sampling for identifying task-specific subgraphs, GML pipeline automation with computational constraints, such as limited time and memory budget, and SPARQLML query optimization. KGNet supports different GML tasks, such as node classification, link prediction, and semantic entity matching. We evaluated KGNet using two real KGs of different application domains. Compared to training on the entire KG, KGNet significantly reduced training time and memory usage while maintaining comparable or improved accuracy. The KGNet source-code is available for further study
summary: This vision paper proposes KGNet, an on-demand graph machine learning (GML) as a service on top of RDF engines to support GML-enabled SPARQL queries. KGNet automates the training of GML models on a KG by identifying a task-specific subgraph. This helps reduce the task-irrelevant KG structure and properties for better scalability and accuracy. While training a GML model on KG, KGNet collects metadata of trained models in the form of an RDF graph called KGMeta, which is interlinked with the relevant subgraphs in KG. Finally, all trained models are accessible via a SPARQL-like query. We call it a GML-enabled query and refer to it as SPARQLML. KGNet supports SPARQLML on top of existing RDF engines as an interface for querying and inferencing over KGs using GML models. The development of KGNet poses research opportunities in several areas, including meta-sampling for identifying task-specific subgraphs, GML pipeline automation with computational constraints, such as limited time and memory budget, and SPARQLML query optimization. KGNet supports different GML tasks, such as node classification, link prediction, and semantic entity matching. We evaluated KGNet using two real KGs of different application domains. Compared to training on the entire KG, KGNet significantly reduced training time and memory usage while maintaining comparable or improved accuracy. The KGNet source-code is available for further study
abstract_zh: 这篇愿景论文提出了 KGNet，这是一种基于 RDF 引擎的按需图机器学习 (GML) 作为服务，以支持支持 GML 的 SPARQL 查询。 KGNet 通过识别特定于任务的子图来自动在 KG 上训练 GML 模型。这有助于减少与任务无关的 KG 结构和属性，以获得更好的可扩展性和准确性。在 KG 上训练 GML 模型时，KGNet 以称为 KGMeta 的 RDF 图的形式收集训练模型的元数据，该图与 KG 中的相关子图相互链接。最后，所有经过训练的模型都可以通过类似 SPARQL 的查询访问。我们将其称为支持 GML 的查询并将其称为 SPARQLML。 KGNet 在现有 RDF 引擎之上支持 SPARQLML，作为使用 GML 模型对 KG 进行查询和推理的接口。 KGNet 的发展为多个领域带来了研究机会，包括用于识别特定任务子图的元采样、具有计算约束（例如有限的时间和内存预算）的 GML 管道自动化，以及 SPARQLML 查询优化。 KGNet支持不同的GML任务，例如节点分类、链接预测和语义实体匹配。我们使用不同应用领域的两个真实 KG 来评估 KGNet。与在整个 KG 上进行训练相比，KGNet 显着减少了训练时间和内存使用量，同时保持了相当或更高的准确性。 KGNet 源代码可供进一步研究

AI.tldr: 本文提出KGNet平台，通过在RDF引擎上提供按需图机器学习（GML）服务，支持GML增强的SPARQL查询。
AI.motivation: 传统知识图谱（KG）上的GML训练存在效率低、可扩展性差的问题，需通过任务相关子图优化模型训练过程。
AI.method: KGNet通过自动识别任务相关子图减少冗余结构，收集模型元数据为RDF图（KGMeta），并支持SPARQLML查询接口。
AI.result: 在两个真实KG上的实验表明，KGNet显著降低训练时间和内存消耗，同时保持或提升模型准确性。
AI.conclusion: KGNet为GML与知识图谱的结合提供了高效框架，未来需在子图采样、资源约束下的流水线自动化及查询优化方面进一步研究。

authors_text: Xuan Shen,Yaohua Wang,Ming Lin,Yilun Huang,Hao Tang,Xiuyu Sun,Yanzhi Wang
title: DeepMAD: Mathematical Architecture Design for Deep Convolutional Neural Network
title_zh: DeepMAD：深度卷积神经网络的数学架构设计
abstract: The rapid advances in Vision Transformer (ViT) refresh the state-of-the-art performances in various vision tasks, overshadowing the conventional CNN-based models. This ignites a few recent striking-back research in the CNN world showing that pure CNN models can achieve as good performance as ViT models when carefully tuned. While encouraging, designing such high-performance CNN models is challenging, requiring non-trivial prior knowledge of network design. To this end, a novel framework termed Mathematical Architecture Design for Deep CNN (DeepMAD) is proposed to design high-performance CNN models in a principled way. In DeepMAD, a CNN network is modeled as an information processing system whose expressiveness and effectiveness can be analytically formulated by their structural parameters. Then a constrained mathematical programming (MP) problem is proposed to optimize these structural parameters. The MP problem can be easily solved by off-the-shelf MP solvers on CPUs with a small memory footprint. In addition, DeepMAD is a pure mathematical framework: no GPU or training data is required during network design. The superiority of DeepMAD is validated on multiple large-scale computer vision benchmark datasets. Notably on ImageNet-1k, only using conventional convolutional layers, DeepMAD achieves 0.7% and 1.5% higher top-1 accuracy than ConvNeXt and Swin on Tiny level, and 0.8% and 0.9% higher on Small level.
summary: The rapid advances in Vision Transformer (ViT) refresh the state-of-the-art performances in various vision tasks, overshadowing the conventional CNN-based models. This ignites a few recent striking-back research in the CNN world showing that pure CNN models can achieve as good performance as ViT models when carefully tuned. While encouraging, designing such high-performance CNN models is challenging, requiring non-trivial prior knowledge of network design. In addition, DeepMAD is a pure mathematical framework: no GPU or training data is required during network design. The superiority of DeepMAD is validated on multiple large-scale computer vision benchmark datasets. Notably on ImageNet-1k, only using conventional convolutional layers, DeepMAD achieves 0.7% and 1.5% higher top-1 accuracy than ConvNeXt and Swin on Tiny level, and 0.8% and 0.9% higher on Small level.
abstract_zh: Vision Transformer (ViT) 的快速进步刷新了各种视觉任务中最先进的性能，使传统的基于 CNN 的模型黯然失色。这引发了 CNN 领域最近一些引人注目的研究，这些研究表明，经过仔细调整，纯 CNN 模型可以实现与 ViT 模型一样好的性能。虽然令人鼓舞，但设计这样的高性能 CNN 模型具有挑战性，需要非常重要的网络设计先验知识。为此，提出了一种称为深度 CNN 数学架构设计 (DeepMAD) 的新颖框架，以有原则的方式设计高性能 CNN 模型。在 DeepMAD 中，CNN 网络被建模为信息处理系统，其表达力和有效性可以通过其结构参数进行分析。然后提出约束数学规划（MP）问题来优化这些结构参数。 MP 问题可以通过内存占用较小的 CPU 上的现成 MP 求解器轻松解决。此外，DeepMAD是一个纯数学框架：网络设计时不需要GPU或训练数据。 DeepMAD的优越性在多个大规模计算机视觉基准数据集上得到了验证。值得注意的是，在 ImageNet-1k 上，仅使用传统的卷积层，DeepMAD 在 Tiny 级别上的 top-1 准确率比 ConvNeXt 和 Swin 高 0.7% 和 1.5%，在 Small 级别上高 0.8% 和 0.9%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DeepMAD框架，通过数学编程方法系统化设计高性能卷积神经网络架构。
AI.motivation: 尽管Vision Transformer（ViT）在视觉任务中表现优异，但近期研究表明精心设计的CNN仍可达到相近性能。然而传统CNN设计依赖非平凡的先验知识，亟需一种系统化方法。
AI.method: 将CNN建模为信息处理系统，通过结构参数的数学表达构建约束数学规划问题，利用CPU上的数学规划求解器进行优化，无需GPU或训练数据。
AI.result: 在ImageNet-1k等大规模数据集上验证，Tiny规模下比ConvNeXt和Swin分别高出0.7%/1.5-top1准确率，Small规模下高出0.8%/0.9%。
AI.conclusion: DeepMAD提供了一种无需训练数据和GPU的数学化CNN设计框架，在多个基准测试中展现出优于现有方法的性能。
